task_language: Pick the milk and place it in the basket
strategy_notes: Localized the milk via two-camera coarse-then-fine: from agentview (image_cam_00 + world_00.npy) I clustered all pixels with z>0.05 in the table region into 5 tall objects + 1 basket; color/height profile let me tentatively label alphabet_soup (front-L can, blue label), cream_cheese (small blue box at xy~+0.15,+0.03 z=0.02), salad_dressing (back-C darkest, very black body), and three red bottles in candidate positions. First two attempts failed: attempt 1 from home pose let Pi0 pick the alphabet_soup can (~xy=+0.08,-0.21) which slipped during carry; attempt 2 (after reset) pre-positioned over the front-center red bottle at xy=+0.05,-0.10 z=0.114 — Pi0 grabbed it with a firm 3.7cm grip, I carried it slowly with gripper=1 and split y-traversal into 0.18m+0.17m hops, released into basket but predicate did NOT fire (so that bottle was ketchup or bbq_sauce, not milk). Attempt 3 (after reset): moved over the back-CL tall bottle at xy=(-0.18,-0.08,0.22) and the WRIST cam (image_wrist_20.png) read RED carton labelled 'Milk' directly below — confirmed visually. pi0_pick 'pick up the milk' lifted it cleanly (peak_lift=0.179m, gripper=5.3cm) and Pi0's chunked trajectory itself carried it across to basket area (post_min_peak_z=0.30 over basket xy~(-0.01,+0.27)). One release primitive then dropped milk into basket and predicate fired. KEY LESSON: agentview alone could not disambiguate which red/dark tall bottle was the milk; wrist-cam closeup with the printed 'Milk' label was the unambiguous signal.
localization_method: agentview world_NN.npy z>0.05 clustering for candidate xy → pre-position eef ~20cm above each candidate → wrist cam (image_wrist_NN.png) classification of object's printed label / shape for ID.
